requirements.description: Exact Claude Fable 5 and Sonnet 5 lanes enabled by the user's private trust policy can edit clean bounded files in the primary Git workspace, skip redundant model review, and still require deterministic verification; every other writer remains isolated.
acceptance_steps: Prove Fable 5 and Sonnet 5 normalize to exact model identities and receive trusted-primary mode only when present in the saved allowlist.
Prove generic Fable, older Sonnet, other providers, dirty owned paths, missing verification, and overlapping ownership fail closed or use isolated worktrees.
Prove successful trusted work is already present in the primary workspace and returns a no-second-model-review receipt backed by deterministic checks.

evidence.ref: node scripts/trusted-primary-regression.js; full fail-fast release suite
evidence.summary: Exact Fable 5 and Sonnet 5 identities require private authorization, clean bounded ownership, deterministic checks, and matching provider receipts; generic or overlapping writers remain isolated, failed direct edits roll back only owned paths, and successful trusted work skips redundant model review.

requirements.description: Codex and Claude Code install AI Mobile from one repository source and expose the same versioned MCP runtime, skill, and bounded worker policy.
acceptance_steps: Validate the root Codex manifest and root Claude Code plugin and marketplace manifests.
Install or update both hosts from the same checkout and prove both resolve the root MCP server and version.
Prove the Claude package does not contain a divergent copied orchestration runtime.

evidence.ref: scripts/install-ai-mobile.ps1; codex plugin list --json; claude plugin list
evidence.summary: Codex and Claude Code both resolve ai-mobile@ai-mobile 1.3.2 from the same repository source and shared MCP runtime after strict manifest validation.

requirements.description: An authorized schema upgrade remains on a capable setup model through validation and installation, then closes only OpenAI.Codex, immediately reopens the exact persisted task, proves the fresh runtime, persists the lightweight console model through thread settings, foregrounds the completed continuation, and resumes useful work through separate workers.
acceptance_steps: Prove a deterministic app-server regression performs initialize, thread/resume, a capable-model verification turn, persistent thread/settings/update, and only after exact runtimeVersion evidence a Luna-low turn in the same thread; stale evidence must stop before Luna.
Commit, push, and install AI Mobile 1.3.2, then prove codex plugin list reports ai-mobile@ai-mobile 1.3.2.

Close only OpenAI.Codex and run the one-shot handoff against this exact task with Sol Ultra verification and GPT-5.6 Luna low continuation; never launch Classic ChatGPT, codex exec resume, a duplicate task, Goal, automation, manager loop, or UI automation.

On the fresh Luna turn, reconcile the existing durable project task once and invoke run-task-cycle exactly once; Luna must not inspect or patch the plugin or project, and the already visible desktop must not wait for coordinator completion.

Prove a real installed provider accepts its structured schema, reaches a terminal state without another user message, is collected once, and either integrates deterministic evidence or emits the correct failure class and recovery transition.

evidence.ref: Prior stale-runtime failure in this exact Codex task
evidence.summary: A Luna-low follow-up appeared in the same task before the upgraded runtime was loaded. It then diagnosed and attempted to repair the stale plugin. This is negative evidence that establishes the required ordering but does not pass the requirement.

Focused regressions prove runtime-before-Luna ordering, persistent Luna-low thread settings, structured child success handling, child-state preservation, and final exact-task foregrounding. User-visible restart proof is still required.

requirements.description: Runtime state is central, concurrent-task safe, branch independent, and provider capacity cannot be rejected from stale negative evidence.
acceptance_steps: Run state tests with two tasks in one workspace and change Git branches between reads.
Cache a provider as unavailable, make it available, and prove dispatch forces a fresh probe before rejection.

evidence.summary: Two central tasks survived a Git branch change without workspace state, and a cached unavailable Claude provider was freshly re-probed and recovered before dispatch.

requirements.description: One natural invocation creates or resumes a finite task whose visible Codex task is a zero-file project console and whose dependency-ready project work is dispatched to separate capacity-selected workers.
acceptance_steps: Start a task without user-designed lanes and prove the coordinator returns a lightweight zero-file console plus a dependency-ready work-plane unit.
Dispatch with omitted workUnits and prove actual project work is assigned to a separate Codex CLI, Claude, Antigravity, or Cursor worker rather than the visible console.
Prove unavailable providers fail closed with a typed recovery transition and do not transfer project ownership to the console.
Collect once and deterministically integrate an isolated patch once with boundaries, concurrent-change protection, verification, rollback, and requirement-specific evidence.

evidence.ref: node scripts/self-test.js; all scripts/*regression.js; all scripts/*e2e.js
evidence.summary: The 46-assertion MCP self-test and complete regression matrix proved coordinator-generated units, separate worker dispatch, console zero-file ownership, finite collection, deterministic one-time integration, conflict protection, rollback, and typed unavailable-provider recovery.

requirements.description: AI Mobile starts no desktop UI, manager loop, Goal, automation, heartbeat, or repeated poll and reports only material transitions.
acceptance_steps: Run startup and inventory tests with process-spawn capture and prove no desktop application is launched.
Inspect the skill and MCP surface for removed manager and polling behavior.

evidence.summary: The ten-tool MCP lifecycle completed without provider or desktop processes, and source assertions found no manager, status-loop, or continuous-cycle tools.

requirements.description: Delegation accounts for prompt, worker, wait, verification, and integration cost and does not spend premium capacity on redundant review.
acceptance_steps: Run small, medium, overlap, premium, and expiring-capacity routing fixtures.
Prove deterministic verification precedes any model review and failed economics produce direct-work guidance.

evidence.summary: Routing kept small work direct, delegated positive medium work, protected the Codex reserve, refused unknown shared Codex capacity, and allowed explicit premium use without a review chain.

requirements.description: One finite portfolio request coordinates multiple independent projects from one capacity inventory while current Codex advances the highest-value critical path and external workers run useful disjoint work concurrently.
acceptance_steps: Coordinate two nontrivial disposable projects with independent outcomes, requirements, priorities, blockers, work graphs, and evidence.
Prove genuinely independent work overlaps in time and one blocked project does not stall useful work in another.
Complete each project only from its own acceptance evidence and complete the portfolio only after all required projects pass.

evidence.summary: One portfolio coordinated two disposable Git projects with overlapping headless workers, current Codex on the highest-priority path, blocked-project independence, project-local evidence, independent completion, and no desktop launch.

requirements.description: Machine-wide provider leases, quota-pool exclusion, worker limits, fairness, Codex reserve, RAM awareness, and file ownership prevent cross-project oversubscription and conflicting edits.
acceptance_steps: Attempt conflicting provider, quota-pool, storage, and file-ownership allocations across projects and prove they fail closed.
Prove unrelated providers and projects can run concurrently within global limits and waiting projects receive fair allocation.
Prove current Codex retains configured reserve and receives the highest-value ready critical path.

evidence.ref: node scripts/global-resource-regression.js; node scripts/economic-regression.js; node scripts/portfolio-e2e.js
evidence.summary: Machine-wide provider, quota-pool, global-worker, file-ownership, fairness, priority, and Codex-reserve gates rejected conflicting allocations while independent providers remained concurrent.

requirements.description: Editing workers use bounded Git worktrees with disk, free-space, age, crash, collection, and cancellation cleanup; read-only workers create no worktree and primary worktrees remain untouched.
acceptance_steps: Prove editing worktrees share Git history, primary worktrees stay unchanged, and read-only workers create no worktree.
Prove collection, cancellation, crash/startup, and maximum-age cleanup remove abandoned worktrees.
Prove disk quota and minimum-free-space gates keep storage within configured limits and transient dependencies, logs, caches, virtual environments, and build outputs are excluded.

evidence.ref: node scripts/storage-lifecycle-regression.js; node scripts/worker-isolation-regression.js; node scripts/global-resource-regression.js
evidence.summary: Read-only worktree count stayed zero; editing worktrees shared Git history, preserved primary files, enforced quota/free-space, excluded transient output, and cleaned on collection, cancellation, lost worker, startup recovery, and maximum age.

requirements.description: AI Mobile discovers bounded authoritative project intent and prevents a method such as review, inspection, or planning from silently replacing the real operational outcome.
acceptance_steps: Create a disposable project with PROJECT_OUTCOME.md and ACCEPTANCE.json whose north star is operational throughput.

Start a task with an incorrectly narrow architecture-review outcome and prove the MCP result resolves to the project north star, imports its acceptance gap, and gives current Codex the first critical-path action.
Prove an explicit latest user request whose final deliverable is a review remains a review and is not overwritten.

evidence.summary: A disposable operational project rejected a method-only review contract, recovered its bounded north star and acceptance gap through stdio MCP, preserved an explicit review deliverable, and returned the first current-Codex critical-path unit.

requirements.description: The latest user correction can revise an existing task or portfolio project, invalidate stale dependent state, and preserve only acceptance evidence that still matches the corrected contract.
acceptance_steps: Revise a task from a diagnostic method to an operational outcome and prove stale rounds, work graph nodes, and mismatched evidence cannot complete the corrected task.
Prove unchanged requirements retain valid evidence and a changed outcome reopens a previously completed task.
Prove active workers are cancelled or revision is refused before a stale contract can continue.

evidence.summary: In-place reconciliation refused an unsafe stale revision, invalidated stale task and portfolio jobs after cancellation, blocked later stale collection, preserved matching evidence, removed changed evidence, and reopened a previously completed task under the corrected contract.

requirements.description: Automatic acceptance-linked decomposition and round results provide a concrete recovery transition after rejection, failure, or collection without polling or a manager loop.
acceptance_steps: Start a task without a work graph and prove one is generated from unresolved acceptance requirements.
Reject an overlapping worker lane and prove the result names the violated invariant, owner, recovery action, and next current-Codex unit.
Collect a terminal round and prove the result returns the next unresolved acceptance-linked action without starting another round or desktop application.

evidence.ref: node scripts/outcome-recovery-e2e.js; node scripts/orchestration-regression.js; node scripts/portfolio-e2e.js
evidence.summary: Acceptance requirements generated a minimal work graph; rejected overlap returned a typed owner, trigger, and recovery action; terminal collection returned exact integration and acceptance identifiers without polling or starting another round.

requirements.description: Normal execution starts or reuses one finite detached event-driven coordinator, returns promptly, uses sandboxed read-only Codex reasoning plus path-bounded patch application for isolated writers, skips verification after missing patches, and never auto-launches a desktop app.
acceptance_steps: Prove run-task-cycle returns a durable coordinator receipt without a visible-turn polling loop and the coordinator completes finite work after the initiating call returns.
Prove isolated Codex writers remain read-only, return a unified diff, and AI Mobile recounts, path-checks, applies, verifies, and integrates it without dangerous sandbox bypass.
Prove a successful provider response with no valid patch fails before any verification command runs.
Prove normal lifecycle and task execution open no desktop application and generated work without explicit files and checks remains read-only.

evidence.ref: node scripts/durable-event-regression.js; node scripts/provider-patch-regression.js; node scripts/task-cycle-regression.js; node scripts/reliability-e2e.js
evidence.summary: A detached coordinator completed after the initiating call, structured observations advanced automatically, malformed hunk counts were safely recounted, out-of-bound patches were rejected, missing patches skipped verification, and no sandbox bypass or desktop launch occurred.

requirements.description: Version 1.3.2 is documented, privacy-safe, scanner compliant, validated for Codex and Claude Code, committed, pushed, and installed from this repository.
acceptance_steps: Run every lifecycle, routing, portfolio, integration, isolation, restart-order, reliability, scanner, and privacy gate.
Validate the Codex and Claude Code plugin manifests and prove both hosts resolve one shared 1.3.2 runtime.
Commit and push the exact verified release, install it locally, and prove codex plugin list reports ai-mobile@ai-mobile 1.3.2.

evidence.summary: All deterministic tests, manifest checks, privacy checks, and scanner profiles pass; commit and push evidence remain pending.

requirements.description: AI Mobile preserves executable blocker transitions, keeps the visible task limited to compact coordination, and immediately assigns the next authorized dependency-ready action to the best eligible work-plane worker with reasoned resource reporting.
acceptance_steps: Start a disposable task with one blocked and one ready requirement and preserve the blocker owner, recovery trigger, and recovery action.
Prove the returned execution contract requires work-plane dispatch while the visible console owns no files and performs no project implementation.
Prove the resource report identifies the console separately from Codex CLI, Claude, Antigravity, and Cursor and explains every selected, idle, reserved, or unavailable provider.
Prove dispatch reports the chosen worker and model, selected acceptance target, and non-selected reasons without asking the user to perform the next safe action.

evidence.ref: node scripts/self-test.js; all scripts/*regression.js; all scripts/*e2e.js
evidence.summary: A lightweight zero-file console returned an immediate work-plane dispatch contract, preserved blockers and resource reasons, and assigned the dependency-ready unit to a separate Claude worker; unavailable providers failed closed without moving implementation into the console.

requirements.description: A durable task whose outcome matches the bounded project outcome refreshes requirement status, blockers, and evidence from the authoritative project acceptance contract without creating a duplicate task or contaminating unrelated work.
acceptance_steps: Prove a project acceptance contract overrides duplicate supplied requirement status unless the latest explicit request declares user outcome authority.
Change project acceptance evidence and prove task-summary refreshes the same durable task without a new task or polling loop.
Prove a task with a different explicit user outcome does not import unrelated project evidence.

evidence.ref: node scripts/outcome-recovery-e2e.js; node scripts/reliability-e2e.js; full 14-suite release matrix
evidence.summary: Matching project acceptance contracts override duplicate stale requirement status; a changed acceptance fingerprint refreshes the same durable task, unchanged project files preserve newer recorded task evidence, and explicit unrelated user outcomes remain isolated.

requirements.description: A finite low-RAM coordinator observes worker termination without an LLM poll, collects each terminal result once, integrates or accepts it once, and advances the next dependency-ready task or portfolio unit without another visible Codex turn.
acceptance_steps: Launch a finite coordinator for a disposable task and prove the MCP call returns promptly while the coordinator continues independently.
Prove worker success, failure, and abrupt process loss all leave running state automatically and create one durable material event.
Prove collection, integration or structured artifact acceptance, and the next dispatch happen exactly once without another visible Codex call.
Prove time, round, token, no-progress, cancellation, crash, RAM, and storage limits stop and clean the coordinator safely.

evidence.summary: A detached deterministic coordinator observed real child-provider termination, collected one terminal result once, accepted or integrated it once, dispatched the dependency-ready successor, and completed acceptance without another visible Codex turn or desktop launch.

requirements.description: Unfamiliar project work is observed through bounded deterministic context and an economical structured artifact before exact writer ownership or verification is committed.
acceptance_steps: Start from an acceptance requirement with no trusted file map and prove the first unit is a bounded read-only observation.
Return a validated compact proposal containing exact files, acceptance criteria, deterministic checks, dependencies, and resource estimates.
Prove the proposal creates the next exact work-graph unit without a second repository-wide model review.
Fingerprint the artifact and prove an unchanged observation cannot rerun.

evidence.summary: A bounded read-only provider returned a structured work plan; AI Mobile validated exact files and deterministic checks, fingerprinted the observation, created one dependency-ready writer node, and completed the verified implementation without prose handoff or duplicate observation.

requirements.description: Provider and model selection uses fresh headless evidence for executables, authentication, models, effort tiers, quota pools, reset horizons, billing mode, reliability, RAM, and capability rather than fixed model names or stale availability.
acceptance_steps: Probe Codex CLI, Claude Code, Antigravity CLI, and optional Cursor without opening desktop applications.
Prove model-specific and shared quota pools cannot be oversubscribed and reset transitions invalidate stale negative capacity.
Prove Windows launches prefer native executables and preserve structured JSON arguments.
Prove launch, transport, timeout, authentication, authorization, capacity, and semantic failures are separately typed.

evidence.ref: node scripts/provider-capability-regression.js; provider-diagnostics-cli passive inventory; Antigravity disposable canary
evidence.summary: Routing uses discovered native model metadata, capability tiers, quota pools, reset horizons, billing/auth mode, Codex reserve, and headless CLI authentication. Live inventory proved that available shared and model-specific capacity, reset timing, authenticated headless providers, and unavailable providers affect routing without publishing the private snapshot.

requirements.description: One portfolio request coordinates two separate nontrivial projects with real headless providers, independent acceptance, concurrent disjoint work, machine-wide leases, fair capacity allocation, and project-local completion evidence.
acceptance_steps: Create two disposable Git projects with independent outcomes, requirements, work graphs, and deterministic checks.
Use at least two installed real headless provider adapters across the portfolio when fresh capacity permits.
Prove useful disjoint work overlaps, one blocked project does not stall the other, and shared quota or file ownership cannot be double leased.
Prove each project and the portfolio complete only from their own accepted evidence with no desktop launch.

evidence.summary: A real Codex and Claude portfolio completed two concurrent disposable projects from isolated project-local acceptance evidence, preserved the 15% Codex reserve, prevented cross-project writes, cleaned all worktrees within storage limits, and opened no desktop applications.

requirements.description: One compact token-efficient status surface reads durable state directly and separates real outcome, plugin runtime, workers, resources, blockers, evidence, and next owned action without a heartbeat or status loop.
acceptance_steps: Return one material status for a task and a portfolio with no repository scan or provider launch.
Show last accepted evidence, active worker and model with routing reason, quota/reset state, blocker owner and recovery, and next already-owned action.
Prove unchanged reads create no events, workers, tasks, model turns, UIs, or progress claims.
Prove status never reports worker existence, process health, or elapsed time as project completion.

evidence.summary: material-status read one bounded coordinator record and deduplicated event journal, separated outcome/progress/workers/resources/blockers/evidence/next, and proved no provider probe, project scan, state mutation, heartbeat, or UI launch.

requirements.description: A privacy-safe diagnostic identifies provider clients, model and endpoint configuration, subscription versus API access, config sources, secret reachability, and the smallest safe canary without exposing credentials.
acceptance_steps: Inspect provider configuration paths and environment-variable names without returning secret values.
Distinguish subscription authentication from API-key billing and report unknown states honestly.
Run the smallest explicitly requested headless canary and record provider, model, category, latency, config source, and quota or rate-limit class.
Prove diagnostics never open a provider UI or print a credential.

evidence.summary: Diagnostics reported executable/authentication/billing/model/quota/capability/config-presence evidence without secret values, then an explicitly requested disposable Antigravity read-only canary returned its exact marker with no desktop UI.

requirements.description: Work units can require source, GitHub, browser, API, local-file, or project-specific capabilities; the coordinator assigns only callable authorized surfaces and returns an explicit user decision when a UI or irreversible action is genuinely required.
acceptance_steps: Route source work to headless workers and deterministic local tools before any UI.
Represent GitHub, browser, API, and project-specific tool requirements in the work-unit contract and reject unavailable capabilities before launch.
Prove authentication-sensitive or irreversible UI actions require explicit authorization and cannot be silently simulated.
Prove normal task and portfolio execution launches no desktop application.

evidence.summary: Capability routing selected only a provider with the required callable browser surface, rejected GitHub when no worker surface exposed it, protected Codex reserve, separated installation from CLI authentication, and produced explicit typed reasons without auto-launching UI.
